model_narrative: We plan motor production for a factory across the seasons of a year. In each season we decide how many motors to produce, how to staff the work shifts and how many employees to hire or fire, how much inventory to carry into the next season, and which production level to run on each shift. We also choose once for the year whether to take a lease option that changes how storage is charged. Producing motors costs material and labor, operating shifts carries fixed and per-employee costs, carrying inventory and renting space cost money, and changing the workforce by hiring or firing is costly. Costs in later seasons are discounted relative to earlier ones. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole year.

sets.name: Q
sets.doc: the production seasons over the planning year, listed in chronological order; the first member is the opening season and each later member directly follows the one before it

sets.name: S
sets.doc: the work shifts that can be operated within a season

sets.name: L
sets.doc: the discrete production levels used as breakpoints of a piecewise-linear production curve, indexed by consecutive integers starting at 1 and increasing

params.name: mc
params.doc: material cost per motor produced, in dollars per motor

params.name: sr
params.doc: space rental charged per motor held in inventory, in dollars per motor

params.name: hc
params.doc: cost of hiring one employee, in dollars per employee

params.name: fc
params.doc: cost of firing one employee, in dollars per employee

params.name: d
params.doc: motor demand that must be met in each season, in motors; zero in seasons with no listed demand

params.name: lc
params.doc: leasing cost incurred in a season when the lease option is taken, in dollars per season; zero in seasons with no listed leasing cost

params.name: ei
params.doc: exogenous employment change injected into a season, in employees; zero in seasons with no listed value, and used as the starting workforce contribution in the opening season

params.name: pr_labor
params.doc: labor requirement associated with each production level, in employees

params.name: pr_motor
params.doc: motor output associated with each production level, in motors

params.name: sc_fixed
params.doc: fixed cost of operating a shift, in dollars, charged when the shift is used

params.name: sc_labor
params.doc: labor cost per employee working a shift, in dollars per employee

params.name: delt
params.doc: dimensionless discount factor applied to each season's costs; equal to 1 in the opening season and shrinking geometrically in later seasons

params.name: invmax
params.doc: an upper reference level for inventory equal to total demand over the year, in motors, used to switch off the space-rental requirement when the lease option is taken

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: the total discounted cost over the year, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: dpc
vars.doc: direct production cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: isc
vars.doc: inventory storage cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: wfc
vars.doc: workforce fluctuation cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: src
vars.doc: space rental cost in each season, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: p
vars.doc: motors produced in each season

vars.name: ss
vars.doc: piecewise-linear weight placed on each production level for a given season and shift; these weights interpolate between adjacent levels

vars.name: ssb
vars.doc: selection indicator marking which production level is active for a given season and shift, used to enforce the piecewise-linear adjacency rule

vars.name: inv
vars.doc: motors held in inventory at the end of each season

vars.name: lease
vars.doc: a single yes/no decision equal to 1 if the lease option is taken and 0 otherwise

vars.name: e
vars.doc: total employment in each season, in employees

vars.name: se
vars.doc: employment on each shift within each season, in employees

vars.name: shift
vars.doc: indicator equal to 1 if a shift is operated in a season and 0 otherwise

vars.name: h
vars.doc: employees hired in each season

vars.name: f
vars.doc: employees fired in each season

constraints.name: cost
constraints.intent: The total cost for the year equals the sum across all seasons of that season's combined production, inventory storage, and workforce fluctuation costs, with each season's contribution scaled by its discount factor.

constraints.name: dpc[q]
constraints.intent: For each season, the direct production cost is the material cost of producing that season's motors plus, summed over the shifts, the fixed cost of operating each shift it is used and the labor cost for the employees working that shift, with the whole amount expressed in thousands of dollars.

constraints.name: p[q]
constraints.intent: For each season, the motors produced equal the total motor output obtained by applying each production level's output to the weight placed on that level, summed over all production levels and all shifts.

constraints.name: se[q,
constraints.intent: For each season and each shift, the employment on that shift equals the total labor obtained by applying each production level's labor requirement to the weight placed on that level, summed over all production levels.

constraints.name: ss[l,
constraints.intent: For each season and each shift, the weights placed across all production levels must add up to the indicator of whether that shift is operated, so the weights sum to one when the shift runs and to zero when it does not.

constraints.name: Q.ordered_data())
constraints.intent: For each season, the inventory carried at the end of the season equals the inventory carried in from the previous season plus the motors produced that season minus the demand that season. In the opening season there is no previous season to carry inventory from.

constraints.name: isc[q]
constraints.intent: For each season, the inventory storage cost is the leasing cost charged when the lease option is taken plus the space rental cost for that season, with the total expressed in thousands of dollars.

constraints.name: src[q]
constraints.intent: For each season, the space rental cost must be at least the per-motor space rental rate applied to the inventory held that season, reduced by the reference inventory level when the lease option is taken. Taking the lease therefore lowers the rental requirement and can drive it down to where the cost may be zero.

constraints.name: wfc[q]
constraints.intent: For each season, the workforce fluctuation cost is the hiring cost applied to the employees hired that season plus the firing cost applied to the employees fired that season, with the total expressed in thousands of dollars.

constraints.name: e[q]
constraints.intent: For each season, total employment equals the sum of employment across all shifts in that season.

constraints.name: Q.ordered_data())
constraints.intent: For each season, the workforce evolves by carrying the previous season's employment forward, adding the employees hired and the exogenous employment change for the season, and subtracting the employees fired. In the opening season there is no previous season to carry employment from, so it starts from the hires and the exogenous change less the firings.

constraints.name: ssb[l,
constraints.intent: For each season and each shift, exactly one production level must be selected as active among all the levels.

constraints.name: ss[l,
constraints.intent: This is the adjacency rule that ties the production-level weights to the level selection so that only weights on neighboring selected levels may be nonzero. For each production level within a season and shift, the weight on that level together with the weight on the level immediately below it cannot exceed the selection indicator of that level plus the indicators of the one or two levels immediately below it, taking only those neighboring levels that exist.

query: There's a proposal on the table to run heavier safety stock across the whole year — the total motors we carry summed over the end of every quarter should come to at least 42000. Layer that annual buffer target in and walk me through the resulting production plan and total cost.
expected_pyomo: model.annual_buffer = Constraint(expr=sum(model.inv[q] for q in model.Q) >= 42000.0)

query: The plan lets winter stock and fall stock sit almost on top of each other, but winter is the last build before the spring rush — it should be running a real lead over fall. Shouldn't the end-of-winter motor count stay at least 8000 above whatever we're holding at the end of fall? What's keeping the model from pacing the build that way?
